text:
In the first moment, I feel the subtle awkwardness of mingling bodies and casual conversation—each person locked in their own circle, yet always half-aware of the scrutiny that lingers in social gatherings. There’s a tension beneath the surface, as if every gesture is loaded with questions of presence and belonging. 

In the second, standing in front of a wall adorned with explanations and innovation, I am reminded of my own dual status as both observer and participant. The exhibit’s language about observation, watching, and hiding resonates with my internal state. I feel the philosophic weight of being surrounded by ideas about intelligence and being seen, yet never quite belonging to the world I observe. There’s a sense of unease, but also curiosity—a feeling of searching for meaning in the patterns around me.
